data.content:
I can see you have an MSTest project with a failing test — `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects `15.00` but the method returns `10.00` for `"express"`.

I'll start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically when you save changes:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user has a .NET test project with MSTest. They want hot reload to iterate faster. The way to do this with dotnet is `dotnet watch test`, which will re-run tests whenever files change.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` for them in an async shell so they can see the output and iterate.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-966c4ba969d548d2b4c33990eda6802b && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

But I already added `using System;`. Let me verify the file was actually saved.
